hints.text: Plates **short, white w/ blue text** (blue hard to see → often looks plain white)
Only **Gen3**, a solid **white car, no antenna** (in Latin America also Peru & Chile)
Buildings often **large red brick w/ an 'unfinished' look** (also common in parts of Peru)
Middle lines yellow / white / **mixed**, outer always white (Chile never mixes; Peru often has outer-only roads)
Concrete-slab + **cobblestone roads** (neat stone lines separating disorganised cobbles in between)
**Bulky white square signposts** (Peru uses black-and-white 'zebra' signposts)
Guardrail markings with **yellow stripes on the outside** (Peru = inverse, black outside)
Poles inconsistent (round concrete w/ pinholes + wooden); **extremely curvy wooden poles** (east) are a great Bolivia clue
km markers = **black top ('Km') / white bottom**, no road number (Argentina's have a road number)
Traditional dress (bowler hats, pollera skirts); **MAS/Evo** graffiti + party colours (black/white/blue); **Wiphala** flag; **Tigo** telecom (not in Peru)

Coverage limited to central & central-west (6 cities: La Paz/El Alto, Cochabamba, Oruro, Sucre, Potosí, Santa Cruz); **Altiplano** plateau (tufted 'hedgehog' grass), lusher north / arider south; reddish soil
